name: Greek Stuffed Footballs
recipeIngredient: 1 (1 Lb.) Loaf Frozen White Bread Dough
recipeInstructions:
1. Bring 3/4 cup Water to a boil in a saucepan. Stir in Rice. Cover, reduce heat and simmer for 40 minutes or until rice is tender and water is absorbed. Remove from heat and set aside.
2. Combine Turkey, Onion, and Garlic in a large skillet. Cook over medium heat until turkey is browned and onion is tender. Remove from heat. Stir in reserved Rice, Oregano, Thyme, Bouillon Granules, Allspice, Pepper, and 1 Egg White, blending well.
3. Thaw Bread Dough according to directions. Place in a large bowl coated with cooking spray, turning once to coat the top. Cover and let rise in a warm place (85 degrees) free from drafts for 1 hour or until doubled in bulk.
4. Combine Egg White and 1 tablespoon Water, blending well.
5. Divide dough into 6 equal portions. Roll each portion into a 7-inch circle. Place half a cup of Turkey Mixture in the center of each circle. Brush edges of dough with Egg White mixture, fold in half, and seal edges. Gently shape into an oblong ball. Place seam side down on a baking sheet coated with cooking spray. Brush tops with Egg White mixture; sprinkle with Sesame Seeds.
6. Bake at 400°F for 18 to 20 minutes or until golden brown. Serve warm or at room temperature. Serve with 3 tablespoons Cucumber-Yogurt Sauce.
